step_by_step_thinking:
Supracondylar fractures of the humerus typically involve displacement of the distal fragment, which tends to be displaced posterolaterally rather than simply anteriorly. The pattern of nerve injuries associated with these fractures can sometimes lead to persistent symptoms due to potential neuropraxia or avulsion injuries.
